title: Employee Data Handling Guidelines – Q1 2026
body:
DOCUMENT ID: comp_edhg_q1_2026_001
EFFECTIVE DATE: March 1, 2026
REVIEW DATE: June 30, 2026
PREPARED BY: Legal and Compliance

===== EXECUTIVE SUMMARY =====

This quarterly compliance note provides practical employee data handling guidelines aligned with GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and SOC2 requirements. This document is intended for all technical and non-technical staff who handle employee personal data in the course of their work, including engineers, developers, HR personnel, and support staff. The guidelines emphasize day-to-day security practices, data minimization, transparency, and rapid incident reporting.

===== 1. SCOPE & APPLICABILITY =====

These guidelines apply to all TechCorp employees, contractors, and service providers who access, process, store, or transmit employee personal data, including names, email addresses, job titles, departments, phone numbers, compensation data, health information, or any other information that identifies or relates to an employee.

As a SaaS provider handling customer data and employee data, TechCorp must comply with GDPR (if processing data of EU-based employees) and maintain SOC2 controls over the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of all sensitive information. Non-compliance carries penalties up to €20 million or 4% of global annual revenue under GDPR, plus reputational and operational damage.

===== 2. GDPR FUNDAMENTALS FOR DAILY WORK =====

2.1 Legal Basis for Processing

Employee data processing relies on specific legal bases, not blanket consent. TechCorp's primary bases are:

- Legitimate Interests: Processing payroll, performance management, and HR administration serves TechCorp's legitimate business interest where the benefit outweighs employee privacy concerns.
- Legal Obligation: Processing tax withholding, statutory benefits, and employment law compliance.
- Employee Consent: Limited to specific, non-coercive purposes (e.g., voluntary wellness program data).

Important: Do not assume
